title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. fibrillosa
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. fibrillosa is a subspecies of the common male fern (Dryopteris filix-mas), a species of fern native to Europe and Asia. This subspecies is characterized by its unique, hairy fronds, which are rich in bioactive compounds, particularly sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. fibrillosa include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
